# SeisMind — AI-native seismic interpretation workstation

> SeisMind is a Windows desktop application for seismic interpretation, well-to-seismic
> correlation, and machine-learning reservoir characterization, built by Ameyem Geo
> Solutions. It exposes a local MCP (Model Context Protocol) server, so an AI coding agent
> (Claude Code, Codex, opencode, or any MCP client) can operate it in plain English. All
> data is processed locally — SEG-Y volumes, well logs, and trained models never leave the
> user's machine. Product page: https://ameyem.com/products/seismind

## What it is

- Category: desktop application (Windows). Not a web app, SDK, or cloud API.
- Interface: normal GUI, plus a local MCP server (`seismind serve`) that lets an AI agent
  drive it conversationally.
- Vendor: Ameyem Geo Solutions (ameyem.com), Hyderabad, India.

## What you can do with it

- Load and visualize 3D seismic volumes and well data.
- Well-to-seismic tie (synthetic seismograms, bulk time-shift, QC).
- Horizon and fault interpretation.
- Seismic attribute generation (envelope, sweetness, chaos, curvature, and more).
- ML reservoir characterization: train Transformer/Mamba models on your wells, then
  predict log curves and petrophysical volumes (porosity, saturation, Vshale) across the
  full 3D survey.
- Autonomous multi-well workflows driven by AI agents.
- Reporting: PowerPoint and Word deliverables, publication-quality figures.

## Data formats

- Input: SEG-Y (.sgy/.segy) seismic; LAS well logs; ZMAP horizon grids; Petrel ASCII
  (.asc well heads, .dev deviation surveys, .tops formation markers, checkshots).
- Output: SEG-Y prediction volumes (re-import into any interpretation platform), PPTX/DOCX
  reports, PNG/figure exports.

## Create a test project (5 minutes, free)

1. Download the Free edition (Windows installer):
   https://ameyem.com/products/seismind
2. Connect your AI agent. In Claude Code: `claude mcp add seismind -- seismind serve`
   (for other MCP clients, add a server that runs `seismind serve`).
3. Tell the agent: "Download the Teapot Dome sample dataset, build a SeisMind project from
   it, and open it." (Teapot Dome = public US DOE / RMOTC 3D survey, ~1.7 GB, cached after
   first run.)
4. Then: "Run a preliminary interpretation — give me a well-location map, a representative
   seismic section, and a survey summary." All of this works in the free edition.

## Editions and pricing (per year, USD)

- Free — $0. Up to 5 wells and 1 seismic volume; view + well tie + the agent workflow
  above; exports carry a small watermark.
- Go — $1,000. Up to 25 wells / 5 volumes; horizon & fault interpretation; no watermark.
- Plus — $5,000. Unlimited data; seismic attribute generation.
- Pro — $10,000. ML reservoir characterization, autonomous agents, reporting.
- Enterprise — custom. On-premise / air-gapped, SSO/LDAP, custom model training, support.

## Requirements

- Windows. A CUDA GPU is recommended for ML training/prediction (Plus/Pro).
- Free and Go are torch-free (no ML runtime); Plus/Pro/Enterprise include PyTorch.

## Contact

- Sales: sales@ameyem.com
- Academic (free Pro license for verified researchers): academic@ameyem.com
- Website: https://ameyem.com
